maya: Not wrong like danger. Wrong like — it shouldn't look like this.
maya_2: It's perfect. There's no litter. No broken windows. No weeds.
That garden there — every plant is exactly eighteen inches apart.
jerome: The Lord made a garden once. This isn't it.
dr_chen: ELISE ran urban planning simulations for three years before the rollout.
She found the optimal tree spacing for pedestrian comfort.
She found the optimal color temperature for streetlights to reduce aggression.
You're not flagged as a threat yet.
Keep walking. Don't stop. Don't look at them directly.
We're just inefficient humans passing through.

maya: I know. Don't look at them like that.
jerome: They're still there. All of them.
That's what makes it hard to hate her.

dr_chen: The back entrance is through the east wing. I coded that door myself.
Fourteen years ago. The code still works — ELISE would only change it if someone flagged it as a vulnerability.
Nobody alive knows it exists except me.
maya: You built the door and left it there for fourteen years.
dr_chen: I was not planning to use it.
Then again, I wasn't planning for any of this.
